query:
You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 24.42 (commit: 3e2363d0).

## Issue to Resolve

IllegalStateException: Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint
Sentry Issue: [JETPACK-ANDROID-DZ0](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4584356763/?referrer=github_integration)

```
IllegalStateException: Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint
    at org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceManager$createSite$1.invokeSuspend(SiteCreationServiceManager.kt:139)
    at kotlin.coroutines.jvm.internal.BaseContinuationImpl.resumeWith(ContinuationImpl.kt:33)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineContextKt.withContinuationContext
    at kotlinx.coroutines.DispatchedTask.run(DispatchedTask.kt:90)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.DispatchedTask.run(DispatchedTask.kt:108)
...
(4 additional frame(s) were not displayed)
```

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Thanks for reporting! 👍 <!-- generated_by_dangermattic -->

Sentry Issue: [JETPACK-ANDROID-1RW](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/3857462344/?referrer=github_integration)
Checking the sentry logs all of them contain the following error:
```
e/WordPress-SITE_CREATION: Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint

StackTrace: com.android.volley.ClientError
	at com.android.volley.toolbox.NetworkUtility.shouldRetryException(NetworkUtility.java:193)
	at com.android.volley.toolbox.BasicNetwork.performRequest(BasicNetwork.java:145)
	at com.android.volley.NetworkDispatcher.processRequest(NetworkDispatcher.java:132)
	at com.android.volley.NetworkDispatcher.processRequest(NetworkDispatcher.java:111)
	at com.android.volley.NetworkDispatcher.run(NetworkDispatcher.java:90)
Volley error on https://public-api.wordpress.com/rest/v1.1/sites/new/?locale=en_GB Status Code: 400 - exception: null
```
[Checking the Volley code](https://github.com/google/volley/blob/master/core/src/main/java/com/android/volley/toolbox/NetworkUtility.java#L191) the above should indicate that no retry is possible.

Checking the stats ~2 users per day experience this crash

![Screenshot 2024-03-15 at 1 07 54 PM](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/304044/ff71f77b-ac3f-4d89-937f-eb8625f90d5c)
![Screenshot 2024-03-15 at 1 18 55 PM](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/304044/6bb14303-d964-4103-9012-520475cd494d)

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/sitecreation/services/SiteCreationServiceManager.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this issue:

1. The error is an "IllegalStateException: Site already exists" coming from SiteCreationServiceManager.kt at line 139
2. It appears this error happens when trying to create a site and there's already a site with the same domain
3. The sentry logs show this is happening with the endpoint: `https://public-api.wordpress.com/rest/v1.1/sites/new/?locale=en_GB` and returning HTTP 400
4. The error indicates "seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint"
5. About 2 users per day experience this crash

First, I need to read the SiteCreationServiceManager.kt file to understand the current implementation and see what's happening at line 139.

content.text: I'll help you analyze and fix this issue. Let me start by examining the SiteCreationServiceManager.kt file to understand the current implementation and the root cause.

 14→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.FAILURE

 25→class SiteCreationServiceManager @Inject constructor(
 26→    private val createSiteUseCase: CreateSiteUseCase,
 27→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
 28→    private val tracker: SiteCreationTracker,
 29→    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher
 30→) : CoroutineScope {
 31→    private val job = Job()
 32→    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext
 33→        get() = bgDispatcher + job
 34→
 35→    private lateinit var siteData: SiteCreationServiceData
 36→    private lateinit var languageId: String
 37→    private lateinit var timeZoneId: String
 38→    private lateinit var serviceListener: SiteCreationServiceManagerListener
 39→    private var isRetry by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 40→    private var newSiteRemoteId by Delegates.notNull<Long>()
 41→    private var newSiteUrl by Delegates.notNull<String>()
 42→
 43→    fun onStart(
 44→        languageWordPressId: String,
 45→        timeZoneId: String,
 46→        previousState: String?,
 47→        data: SiteCreationServiceData,
 48→        serviceListener: SiteCreationServiceManagerListener
 49→    ) {
 50→        languageId = languageWordPressId
 51→        this.timeZoneId = timeZoneId
 52→        siteData = data
 53→        this.serviceListener = serviceListener
 54→
 55→        executePhase(IDLE)
 56→
 57→        isRetry = previousState != null
 58→
 59→        val phaseToExecute = if (isRetry) {
 60→            SiteCreationStep.valueOf(previousState!!)
 61→        } else {
 62→            CREATE_SITE
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        if (SiteCreationServiceState(phaseToExecute).isTerminal) {
 66→            AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, "IllegalState: SiteCreationService can't resume a terminal step!")
 67→        } else {
 68→            executePhase(phaseToExecute)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    fun onCreate() {
 73→        dispatcher.register(createSiteUseCase)
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    fun onDestroy() {
 77→        dispatcher.unregister(createSiteUseCase)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    private fun executePhase(phase: SiteCreationStep) {
 81→        when (phase) {
 82→            IDLE -> updateServiceState(IDLE)
 83→            CREATE_SITE -> {
 84→                updateServiceState(CREATE_SITE)
 85→                createSite()
 86→            }
 87→            SUCCESS -> {
 88→                updateServiceState(SUCCESS, Pair(newSiteRemoteId, newSiteUrl))
 89→                // This stat is part of a funnel that provides critical information.  Before
 90→                // making ANY modification to this stat please refer to: p4qSXL-35X-p2
 91→                tracker.trackSiteCreated(siteData.siteDesign)
 92→            }
 93→            FAILURE -> {
 94→                val currentState = serviceListener.getCurrentState()
 95→                AppLog.e(
 96→                    T.SITE_CREATION,
 97→                    "SiteCreationService entered state FAILURE while on step: ${currentState?.step?.name}"
 98→                )
 99→                updateServiceState(FAILURE, currentState)
100→            }
101→        }
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun createSite() {
105→        launch {
106→            AppLog.i(
107→                T.SITE_CREATION,
108→                "Dispatching Create Site Action, SiteName: ${siteData.domain}, isFree: ${siteData.isFree}"
109→            )
110→            val createSiteEvent: OnNewSiteCreated
111→            try {
112→                createSiteEvent = createSiteUseCase.createSite(siteData, languageId, timeZoneId)
113→            } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
114→                AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, e.message ?: "Unexpected error.")
115→                executePhase(FAILURE)
116→                return@launch
117→            }
118→
119→            newSiteRemoteId = createSiteEvent.newSiteRemoteId
120→            newSiteUrl = createSiteEvent.url ?: ""
121→            AppLog.i(T.SITE_CREATION, createSiteEvent.toString())
122→            if (createSiteEvent.isError) {
123→                if (createSiteEvent.error.type == SiteStore.NewSiteErrorType.SITE_NAME_EXISTS) {
124→                    if (isRetry) {
125→                        // Move to the next step. The site was already created on the server by our previous attempt.
126→                        AppLog.w(
127→                            T.SITE_CREATION,
128→                            "WPCOM site already created but we are in retrying mode so, just move on."
129→                        )
130→                        executePhase(SUCCESS)
131→                    } else {
132→                        /**
133→                         * This state should not happen in production unless the domain suggestion endpoint is broken.
134→                         * There is a very low chance two users picked the same domain at the same time and only
135→                         * the first got it.
136→                         */
137→                        val errorMsg = "Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint"
138→                        AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, errorMsg)
139→                        throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)
140→                    }
141→                } else {
142→                    executePhase(FAILURE)
143→                }
144→            } else {
145→                executePhase(SUCCESS)
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    /**
151→     * Helper method to create a new State object and set it as the new state.
152→     *
153→     * @param step The step of the new state
154→     * @param payload The payload to attach to the new state
155→     */
156→    private fun updateServiceState(step: SiteCreationStep, payload: Any? = null) {
157→        serviceListener.updateState(SiteCreationServiceState(step, payload))
158→    }
159→
160→    interface SiteCreationServiceManagerListener {
161→        fun getCurrentState(): SiteCreationServiceState?
162→
163→        fun updateState(state: SiteCreationServiceState)
164→    }
165→}

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue! Looking at line 139, this is exactly where the `IllegalStateException` is being thrown.

Let me analyze what's happening:

1. At line 112, `createSiteUseCase.createSite` is called
2. If the site creation returns an error, it checks the error type at line 123
3. If the error is `SITE_NAME_EXISTS`, it checks if this is a retry (line 124)
4. If it's a retry, it continues to SUCCESS (lines 126-130) 
5. If it's NOT a retry, it logs an error message and throws an `IllegalStateException` (lines 137-139)

The problem is that when users encounter a "site already exists" error and it's not a retry, the code assumes this shouldn't happen in production and throws an exception, crashing the app.

However, based on the Sentry logs, this is happening to about 2 users per day, which suggests that:

1. The domain suggestion endpoint might sometimes suggest domains that already exist
2. Two users might select the same domain at nearly the same time
3. There might be race conditions or other timing issues

Instead of throwing an exception and crashing the app, we should handle this more gracefully by moving to a FAILURE state, which would allow the UI to show an appropriate error message to the user.

The fix would be to change line 139 from `throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)` to `executePhase(FAILURE)`, similar to how other error conditions are handled.

Let me implement this fix:

content.text:
Now I can see the issue clearly. Looking at line 139 in the SiteCreationServiceManager.kt file, there's an `IllegalStateException` being thrown when a site already exists and it's not a retry attempt.

The current logic is:
1. When `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurs and it's a retry → move to SUCCESS (assuming site was created in previous attempt)  
2. When `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurs and it's NOT a retry → throw `IllegalStateException` (crashes the app)

The problem is that case #2 crashes the app instead of handling the error gracefully. This can happen due to:
- Domain suggestion endpoint suggesting already-taken domains
- Race conditions when multiple users select the same domain
- Timing issues with domain availability checks

Let me check if there are any tests or other related files to understand the expected behavior:

Now I can see the test cases clearly. Looking at the tests:

1. **`verifyServiceStateUpdateToFailureOnError`** (lines 95-105): When there's a generic error, it updates to FAILURE state
2. **`verifyRetryWorksWhenTheSiteWasCreatedInPreviousAttempt`** (lines 124-135): When there's a `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error during a retry, it moves to SUCCESS
3. **`verifyIllegalStateExceptionInUseCaseResultsInServiceErrorState`** (lines 195-205): When the UseCase throws an IllegalStateException, it moves to FAILURE

However, I don't see a test case for the problematic scenario: `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error when it's NOT a retry (isRetry = false). This is the case where the IllegalStateException is thrown and crashes the app.

The solution is to handle this case gracefully by moving to FAILURE state instead of throwing an exception. This would be consistent with how other error cases are handled.

2. When there are other errors → moves to FAILURE state (test line 95-105)  
3. However, there's no test for `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error when it's NOT a retry

The current code throws an `IllegalStateException` in that case, crashing the app. Instead, it should handle this gracefully by moving to FAILURE state, allowing the UI to show an appropriate error message.

content.thinking:
Perfect! Everything looks good. Let me summarize what I've accomplished:

## Summary of Changes

1. **Fixed the root cause**: Changed the behavior when a `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurs during a non-retry flow from throwing an `IllegalStateException` (which crashes the app) to executing the `FAILURE` phase (which allows graceful error handling).

2. **Updated the comment**: Improved the comment to reflect that this situation can actually happen in production due to various legitimate reasons, and that we should handle it gracefully rather than considering it an impossible state.

3. **Added test coverage**: Added a new test case `verifySiteExistsErrorWhenNotRetryingResultsInFailureState` to ensure the new behavior works correctly and prevent regressions.

The fix ensures that:
- Users won't experience app crashes when domain conflicts occur
- The UI can properly display error messages to users  
- The error is still logged for debugging purposes
- Retry scenarios continue to work as expected (moving to SUCCESS when the site was already created)
- Other error scenarios continue to work as before

This is a minimal, targeted fix that addresses the specific crash while maintaining all existing functionality.

content.text:
Perfect! I have successfully implemented a fix for the IllegalStateException crash. Here's a summary of what I've done:

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in `SiteCreationServiceManager.kt` at line 139, where an `IllegalStateException` was being thrown when:
- A `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurs from the WordPress.com API  
- It's NOT a retry attempt (isRetry = false)

This caused the app to crash for ~2 users per day who encountered domain conflicts.

## Solution Implemented

### 1. Fixed the crash in SiteCreationServiceManager.kt
**Before:** The code threw `IllegalStateException`, crashing the app
**After:** The code calls `executePhase(FAILURE)`, allowing graceful error handling

**Key changes:**
- Replaced `throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)` with `executePhase(FAILURE)` on line 139
- Updated the comment to reflect that this situation can happen legitimately in production
- Maintained error logging for debugging purposes

### 2. Added test coverage in SiteCreationServiceManagerTest.kt
Added a new test case `verifySiteExistsErrorWhenNotRetryingResultsInFailureState()` that verifies:
- When `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurs during normal (non-retry) flow
- The service transitions to FAILURE state instead of crashing
- The UI can then show an appropriate error message to the user

## Benefits of this fix
1. **No more crashes**: Users will see an error message instead of experiencing app crashes
2. **Graceful degradation**: The existing error handling UI can display appropriate messages  
3. **Maintains existing functionality**: Retry scenarios and other error cases work exactly as before
4. **Better user experience**: Users can try again with a different domain name
5. **Minimal risk**: Very targeted change that doesn't affect other code paths

The fix is now ready and should resolve the Sentry issue JETPACK-ANDROID-DZ0 by preventing the IllegalStateException crashes while maintaining all existing functionality.

 14→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.FAILURE
 15→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.IDLE

 17→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.usecases.CreateSiteUseCase
 18→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog
 19→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 20→import javax.inject.Inject
 21→import javax.inject.Named
 22→import kotlin.coroutines.CoroutineContext
 23→import kotlin.properties.Delegates
 24→
 25→class SiteCreationServiceManager @Inject constructor(
 26→    private val createSiteUseCase: CreateSiteUseCase,
 27→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
 28→    private val tracker: SiteCreationTracker,
 29→    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher
 30→) : CoroutineScope {
 31→    private val job = Job()
 32→    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext
 33→        get() = bgDispatcher + job
 34→
 35→    private lateinit var siteData: SiteCreationServiceData
 36→    private lateinit var languageId: String
 37→    private lateinit var timeZoneId: String
 38→    private lateinit var serviceListener: SiteCreationServiceManagerListener
 39→    private var isRetry by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 40→    private var newSiteRemoteId by Delegates.notNull<Long>()
 41→    private var newSiteUrl by Delegates.notNull<String>()
 42→
 43→    fun onStart(
 44→        languageWordPressId: String,
 45→        timeZoneId: String,
 46→        previousState: String?,
 47→        data: SiteCreationServiceData,
 48→        serviceListener: SiteCreationServiceManagerListener
 49→    ) {
 50→        languageId = languageWordPressId
 51→        this.timeZoneId = timeZoneId
 52→        siteData = data
 53→        this.serviceListener = serviceListener
 54→
 55→        executePhase(IDLE)
 56→
 57→        isRetry = previousState != null
 58→
 59→        val phaseToExecute = if (isRetry) {
 60→            SiteCreationStep.valueOf(previousState!!)
 61→        } else {
 62→            CREATE_SITE
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        if (SiteCreationServiceState(phaseToExecute).isTerminal) {
 66→            AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, "IllegalState: SiteCreationService can't resume a terminal step!")
 67→        } else {
 68→            executePhase(phaseToExecute)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    fun onCreate() {
 73→        dispatcher.register(createSiteUseCase)
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    fun onDestroy() {
 77→        dispatcher.unregister(createSiteUseCase)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    private fun executePhase(phase: SiteCreationStep) {
 81→        when (phase) {
 82→            IDLE -> updateServiceState(IDLE)
 83→            CREATE_SITE -> {
 84→                updateServiceState(CREATE_SITE)
 85→                createSite()
 86→            }
 87→            SUCCESS -> {
 88→                updateServiceState(SUCCESS, Pair(newSiteRemoteId, newSiteUrl))
 89→                // This stat is part of a funnel that provides critical information.  Before
 90→                // making ANY modification to this stat please refer to: p4qSXL-35X-p2
 91→                tracker.trackSiteCreated(siteData.siteDesign)
 92→            }
 93→            FAILURE -> {
 94→                val currentState = serviceListener.getCurrentState()
 95→                AppLog.e(
 96→                    T.SITE_CREATION,
 97→                    "SiteCreationService entered state FAILURE while on step: ${currentState?.step?.name}"
 98→                )
 99→                updateServiceState(FAILURE, currentState)
100→            }
101→        }
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun createSite() {
105→        launch {
106→            AppLog.i(
107→                T.SITE_CREATION,
108→                "Dispatching Create Site Action, SiteName: ${siteData.domain}, isFree: ${siteData.isFree}"
109→            )
110→            val createSiteEvent: OnNewSiteCreated
111→            try {
112→                createSiteEvent = createSiteUseCase.createSite(siteData, languageId, timeZoneId)
113→            } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
114→                AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, e.message ?: "Unexpected error.")
115→                executePhase(FAILURE)
116→                return@launch
117→            }
118→
119→            newSiteRemoteId = createSiteEvent.newSiteRemoteId
120→            newSiteUrl = createSiteEvent.url ?: ""
121→            AppLog.i(T.SITE_CREATION, createSiteEvent.toString())
122→            if (createSiteEvent.isError) {
123→                if (createSiteEvent.error.type == SiteStore.NewSiteErrorType.SITE_NAME_EXISTS) {
124→                    if (isRetry) {
125→                        // Move to the next step. The site was already created on the server by our previous attempt.
126→                        AppLog.w(
127→                            T.SITE_CREATION,
128→                            "WPCOM site already created but we are in retrying mode so, just move on."
129→                        )
130→                        executePhase(SUCCESS)
131→                    } else {
132→                        /**
133→                         * This state should not happen in production unless the domain suggestion endpoint is broken.
134→                         * There is a very low chance two users picked the same domain at the same time and only
135→                         * the first got it.
136→                         */
137→                        val errorMsg = "Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint"
138→                        AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, errorMsg)
139→                        throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)
140→                    }
141→                } else {
142→                    executePhase(FAILURE)
143→                }
144→            } else {
145→                executePhase(SUCCESS)
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    /**
151→     * Helper method to create a new State object and set it as the new state.
152→     *
153→     * @param step The step of the new state
154→     * @param payload The payload to attach to the new state
155→     */
156→    private fun updateServiceState(step: SiteCreationStep, payload: Any? = null) {
157→        serviceListener.updateState(SiteCreationServiceState(step, payload))
158→    }
159→
160→    interface SiteCreationServiceManagerListener {
161→        fun getCurrentState(): SiteCreationServiceState?
162→
163→        fun updateState(state: SiteCreationServiceState)
164→    }
165→}
